name: MicroStrategy Analytics & Intelligence API
what_it_does: MicroStrategy REST API for enterprise business intelligence and analytics platform. Enables AI agents to manage report and dashboard retrieval and execution for enterprise analytics automation, handle user and group management for BI governance and access control, access document and report publishing workflow data for analytics distribution, retrieve security filter and data access policy data for row-level security management, handle project and object metadata retrieval for BI catalog management, access drill-down and prompt handling for interactive analytics automation, retrieve cube and dataset data for analytics data pipeline integration, manage subscription and delivery scheduling for automated report distribution, handle MicroStrategy Library (mobile analytics) data for self-service analytics, and integrate BI data with data warehouse, ETL, and enterprise applications.
use_cases: Executing reports from analytics automation agents
Managing user provisioning from BI governance agents
Distributing dashboards from scheduled delivery agents
Retrieving analytics data from data pipeline agents
not_for: Simple data queries without enterprise BI governance requirements
Modern cloud-native BI without legacy MicroStrategy architecture
SMB analytics without enterprise scale BI platform complexity
best_when: A large enterprise, financial institution, or government agency using MicroStrategy wants AI agents to automate report execution, analytics data retrieval, user provisioning, dashboard delivery, and BI catalog management.
avoid_when: COMPLIANCE RISK: MicroStrategy manages enterprise analytics with governed data access — automated row-level security bypass or security filter overrides creates unauthorized data exposure. Report distribution automation must respect data classification and recipient authorization. MicroStrategy may contain PII or regulated financial data in analytics outputs; automated data export without data loss prevention controls creates compliance exposure.

auth.notes: MicroStrategy uses session-based authentication (login tokens) and OAuth 2.0. REST API docs on GitHub. Tysons Corner, Virginia HQ. Public (MSTR NASDAQ). MicroStrategy CEO Michael Saylor is a major Bitcoin advocate; company holds significant BTC treasury. MicroStrategy ONE is the current platform (formerly MicroStrategy 2021). Strong in financial services, healthcare, and government BI. No webhooks — polling-based report execution. Competes with Tableau, Power BI, and Qlik for enterprise BI.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription by user type (developer, analyst, viewer) and deployment
pricing.notes: Tysons Corner, Virginia. Founded 1989. Public (MSTR NASDAQ). $500M+ revenue. MicroStrategy ONE (formerly MicroStrategy 2021). Bitcoin treasury strategy (CEO Michael Saylor). Strong in large enterprise BI, financial services. HyperIntelligence (browser extension analytics). Embedded analytics SDK. Competes with Tableau (Salesforce), Power BI (Microsoft), and Qlik for enterprise BI.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per session token; server configuration dependent

known_agent_gotchas: COMPLIANCE RISK: MicroStrategy row-level security filters control data access; automated security filter bypass or override creates unauthorized data exposure for sensitive reports
Session token management — MicroStrategy uses login tokens (not persistent OAuth tokens); automated agents must handle token expiration and re-authentication within session lifetime

Report execution latency — complex MicroStrategy reports with large datasets can take seconds to minutes to execute; automated polling must handle asynchronous report execution with appropriate timeout
Bitcoin strategy distraction — CEO Michael Saylor's Bitcoin advocacy and corporate BTC strategy creates company strategic uncertainty; verify R&D investment and product roadmap independently
On-premise installation base — MicroStrategy has large on-premise installed base; cloud (MicroStrategy ONE SaaS) and on-premise have different API endpoints and authentication
Prompt handling complexity — many MicroStrategy reports require prompt answers before execution; automated report execution must handle required prompt parameters correctly
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise BI analytics. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. Session token/OAuth2. US/EU/on-premise. Enterprise analytics and governed data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise BI. SOC2, FedRAMP. Session token/OAuth2. US/EU. Enterprise analytics data.
